title: function...end
html:
function...end

Declare a ProcScript function, a script module that can be invoked from other modules in its vicinity.

function FunctionName
{returns DataType}
{params
...
endparams}
{variables
...
endvariables}

... Proc statements and precompiler directives

{return (Expression) }
end
        

Parameters

FunctionName—literal name of the function; can have a maximum length of 32 bytes; these characters can be letters (A-Z), digits (0-9), or underscores (_).

Return Values

The function statement does not affect the value of $status.

If return is specified, the value is returned inline. This value can be used when the function is called as a function, but not when it is called using the call statement.

Use

Allowed in all component types, as well as application shells and as global ProcScripts.

Description

The function statement declares the name and the starting point of a function. The visibility of the function depends on where it is declared:
  • A function that is declared in a field’s code container can be called from other modules that exist at the level of that field.
  • A function that is declared in an entity’s code container can be called from other modules that exist at the level of that entity, as well as from modules that exist at the level of the entity’s fields.
  • A function that is declared in a component code container can be called from all modules that exist anywhere in the component, including modules in all entities and fields.

If there is a module declaration that precedes the function declaration, that preceding module must end with an explicit end statement.

Functions in Global ProcScript

The Global ProcScripts are implicit functions, but they can also contain explicitly-defined functions. These must be placed at the end of all other code in the global ProcScript.

Functions that are declared in Global ProcScripts can be called from that Global ProcScript and from other ProcScript modules that are declared in that same Global ProcScript.

Calling Functions

Functions can be explicitly called using the call ProcScript statement, in which case any return value is returned in $status.

If they are called as a function, the return value can be assigned to a variable or used as an argument or parameter. This means that they can be used directly in the code.

It also means that the return value can be of a different data type than numeric (which is what $status is).

For example:

function doSomething
 returns string ; set the data type of the return value 
 return("I did something") ; set the return value
end

This can be called:

  • As a function: 
    vResult = doSomething()
; vResult = "I did something"
; $status = 0
  • Using call: 
    call doSomething
; $status = 0

    In this case, the return value is not captured inline.

Example: Using Functions

In the following example, the store trigger contains a call to a function in the component.

trigger store
call LSTORE
end; store

; Component-level Script container
function LSTORE
 store
 if ($status < 0)
 message "Store error!"
 rollback
 else
 message "Store done."
 commit
 endif
end

The following code calls function multiply and assigns its value to TOTAL:

TOTAL = multiply(FLD1, FLD2)

; Component-level Script container
function multiply
returns numeric
params
 numeric parm1 : IN
 numeric parm2 : IN
endparams
variables
 numeric multiplyResult
endvariables
multiplyResult = parm1 * parm2
return multiplyResult
end ; multiply
Related Links
  • call

    Execute the specified function or global ProcScript.

  • params…endparams

    Declare the parameter block for an operation, function, or global ProcScript.

  • variables…endvariables

    Define the variables for a ProcScript module or component.

  • Functions

    Functions are ProcScript modules defined by the function ProcScript statement. They can be used as private functions, or they can be executed in response to a call statement.
